title: 10 best herbal teas for frozen shoulder
intro_desc: Herbal teas for frozen shoulder are infusions made from plants that help alleviate the symptoms of this condition. Frozen shoulder, also known as adhesive capsulitis, is a painful and debilitating disorder that restricts movement in the shoulder joint. Certain herbal teas, such as ginger, turmeric, and willow bark, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the shoulder joint, thereby alleviating pain and improving mobility. For example, drinking ginger tea twice a day can help reduce inflammation and improve range of motion, allowing individuals to perform daily activities with greater ease and comfort. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals with frozen shoulder can experience a significant improvement in their quality of life, with reduced pain and increased mobility.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tea helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the affected area. The tea is made from the dried flowers of the Arnica montana plant, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various musculoskeletal conditions. Arnica is believed to stimulate blood flow and promote healing, making it an ideal natural remedy for frozen shoulder. The tea can help improve mobility and reduce stiffness in the shoulder joint, allowing for a greater range of motion and increased comfort. Regular consumption of arnica flower tea may help manage the symptoms of frozen shoulder and promote overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tea helps reduce inflammation in the shoulder, providing pain relief and improving mobility, due to its active constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.

Analgesic: Arnica flower tea alleviates pain associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are involved in pain signaling, thanks to its helenalin and dihydrohelenalin content.
Circulation-enhancing: Arnica flower tea promotes blood flow to the affected area, facilitating healing and reducing stiffness, owing to its thymol and carvacrol content.
Antioxidant: Arnica flower tea's antioxidant properties help protect cells from damage, supporting tissue repair and reducing inflammation, due to its high flavonoid and phenolic acid content.
Muscle-relaxant: Arnica flower tea helps relax muscles, reducing tension and spasms in the shoulder, as a result of its active constituents such as isobutyl angelate and isovaleric acid.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Arnica flowers are commonly used in medicinal tea for frozen shoulder due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the arnica plant are used in tea to help improve mobility and reduce stiffness in the shoulder, making it easier to move.
Rhyzomes: Arnica rhyzomes are rich in compounds that can help improve blood flow, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the shoulder.
Buds: Arnica buds are often used in tea for their potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and promote recovery in cases of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of fresh arnica flowers with 4 cups of water, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let the tea simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the arnica flowers, then allow it to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in honey to taste, if desired, for added sweetness.
Drink 1 cup of the arnica flower tea, 3 times a day, for relief of frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Safflower tea helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area. The tea is also known to improve blood circulation, which can help alleviate stiffness and promote mobility in the shoulder. Additionally, safflower tea contains compounds that can help relieve pain, making it easier for individuals with frozen shoulder to perform daily activities. Regular consumption of safflower tea can help manage the symptoms of frozen shoulder and promote overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Safflower tea helps reduce inflammation in the shoulder, providing pain relief and improving mobility, due to its active constituents, including safflomin A and Ligustilide.
Improves blood circulation: Safflower tea enhances blood flow to the shoulder, promoting healing and reducing stiffness, thanks to its richness in Linoleic acid.
Analgesic: Safflower tea acts as a natural painkiller, alleviating discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, owing to the presence of active components like Safranal.

Antioxidant: Safflower tea contains antioxidants that help protect cells from damage, supporting overall joint health and accelerating recovery from frozen shoulder, due to its high concentration of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Muscle relaxant: Safflower tea has muscle-relaxing properties, which help ease tension in the shoulder and improve range of motion, attributed to the action of active constituents like Atractylenolide I and II.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, safflower flowers help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which may alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.
Seeds: High in linoleic acid, safflower seeds can help reduce inflammation and promote joint mobility, making them beneficial for treating frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Containing several essential vitamins and minerals, safflower leaves can help improve overall joint health and reduce stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 2 cups of water and steep 2 teaspoons of dried safflower flowers for 10-15 minutes.
Steep - After boiling, remove from heat and add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers, letting it steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain - Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used flowers.
Sweeten - Add honey to taste, if desired, for a more soothing effect.
Sip - Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tea helps with frozen shoulder because it contains high levels of vitamin C and other antioxidants that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area. The capsaicin found in bell peppers also has natural pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, the warmth of the tea can help to relax muscles and improve circulation, further aiding in the healing process. Regular consumption of bell pepper tea may help to reduce the severity of symptoms and improve overall joint mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper tea contains capsaicin, which helps reduce inflammation in the shoulder, providing pain relief and promoting mobility.
Analgesic: The capsaicin in bell pepper tea also has pain-relieving properties, helping to alleviate the discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
Antioxidant: Bell pepper tea is rich in vitamin C and other antioxidants, which support collagen production and tissue repair in the shoulder joint.
Circulation-boosting: The niacin (vitamin B3) in bell pepper tea promotes blood flow, helping to ensure that nutrients reach the shoulder joint and support healing.
Immune-boosting: Bell pepper tea's high vitamin C content strengthens the immune system, which can help the body recover from the inflammation and pain caused by frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, the flowers can improve blood circulation and promote tissue healing in the shoulder joint.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils and nutrients, the seeds can help alleviate stiffness and improve mobility in the shoulder.
Stems: Contain beneficial polyphenols and tannins that can help reduce inflammation and strengthen the shoulder muscles.
remedy_recipe: Chop one bell pepper and two sprigs of rosemary, finely crushing 1 teaspoon of dried lavender flowers.
Combine 2 cups of water, chopped bell pepper, rosemary, and lavender in a saucepan, bringing to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, simmer for 15 minutes to allow herbs to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding solids.
Drink the tea while warm, three times a day for two weeks as a natural remedy for frozen shoulder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Climbing staff tree tea helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate the pain and inflammation associated with the condition. The herb, also known as Tripterygium wilfordii, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including joint pain and stiffness. The tea made from the climbing staff tree has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can help reduce the swelling and stiffness in the shoulder joint. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the joint from further damage, promoting healing and improving mobility. Overall, climbing staff tree tea can be a natural and effective way to manage the symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Climbing staff tree tea can help alleviate the pain associated with frozen shoulder by containing analgesic compounds such as alkaloids, which interfere with pain signaling in the body.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of climbing staff tree tea, due to compounds like flavonoids and tannins, can help reduce inflammation in the shoulder joint, thereby improving mobility.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, provided by polyphenols, helps protect cells from damage, promoting tissue repair and reducing oxidative stress in the shoulder area.

Circulation-enhancing: Climbing staff tree tea can improve blood flow to the shoulder joint, thanks to active constituents like essential oils and saponins, which help dilate blood vessels and promote circulation.

Muscle-relaxing: The muscle-relaxing properties of climbing staff tree tea, attributed to alkaloids and flavonoids, can help alleviate tension in the shoulder muscles, reducing stiffness and improving range of motion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting mobility in frozen shoulder.
Buds: Rich in essential oils, which have been shown to improve blood circulation and alleviate pain, making them beneficial for treating frozen shoulder.
Stems: Contain therapeutic compounds that can help reduce stiffness and improve joint mobility, making them useful for treating frozen shoulder.
Flowers: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
Bark: Contains tannins and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it useful for treating painful conditions like frozen shoulder.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Collect 2-3 leaves from a Climbing Staff Tree (Senegalia rugata) and rinse them thoroughly.
Crush the leaves gently to release their essential oils.
Place the crushed leaves in a cup and pour boiling water over them.
Steep the leaves for 5-10 minutes, then strain out the solids.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day, for at least a week for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the shoulder joint, thereby alleviating pain and stiffness. Additionally, cinnamon is known to improve circulation and increase blood flow, which can help promote healing and mobility in the affected area. The warm temperature of the tea can also provide soothing relief to tight and stiff muscles, making it an effective natural remedy for frozen shoulder. However, it is important to note that while cinnamon tea can help manage the symptoms of frozen shoulder, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or physical therapy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cinnamon tea helps reduce inflammation in the shoulder, providing pain relief and improving mobility, due to the presence of compounds such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.

Analgesic: The tea's ability to alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder is attributed to its analgesic properties, which can be traced back to active constituents like cinnamaldehyde and coumarin.

Antioxidant: Cinnamon tea contains high levels of antioxidants that help combat oxidative stress and support tissue repair, contributing to the healing process in frozen shoulder; key antioxidants include polyphenols, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Improves Circulation: The tea promotes blood flow and circulation to the affected area, aiding in the healing process by ensuring essential nutrients and oxygen reach the shoulder joint; this is facilitated by the presence of compounds like cinnamaldehyde and coumarin.

Immune-boosting: Cinnamon tea can help strengthen the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections and inflammation, thus aiding in the recovery from frozen shoulder; active constituents responsible for these immune-boosting properties include cinnamaldehyde, polyphenols, and vitamin C.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the cinnamon tea plant is commonly used in medicinal teas for frozen shoulder due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area.
Leaves: The leaves of the cinnamon tea plant are often used in teas for their antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the shoulder joint.

Seeds: The seeds of the cinnamon tea plant contain essential oils that have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Roots: The roots of the cinnamon tea plant are used in medicinal teas for their ability to improve blood circulation, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the shoulder joint.

Stems: The stems of the cinnamon tea plant contain compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them useful for treating the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of crushed cinnamon sticks, allowing it to simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers and let it steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the solid particles and allow it to cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of raw honey for added anti-inflammatory benefits and to improve the taste.
Drink the herbal tea, twice a day, for several weeks to help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with frozen shoulder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with frozen shoulder because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation and swelling in the affected shoulder joint. The antioxidant properties of curcumin also help to repair damaged tissues and promote joint mobility. Moreover, turmeric tea's warmth can provide soothing relief to stiff and aching shoulders, enhancing blood flow and alleviating pain. Regular consumption of turmeric tea can improve flexibility, reduce stiffness, and promote overall shoulder health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Curcumin, a key active ingredient in turmeric, helps reduce inflammation in frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals such as COX-2 and 5-LOX.

Pain-relieving: Turmeric's analgesic properties, primarily attributed to curcumin, help alleviate pain in frozen shoulder by blocking the pain receptors in the brain and reducing the sensation of discomfort.

Improves mobility: Turmeric's ability to enhance joint mobility, partly due to its anti-inflammatory effects, helps restore range of motion in frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and stiffness in the affected joint.

Antioxidant: Turmeric's high antioxidant content, mainly from curcumin, helps protect cells from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to the development and progression of frozen shoulder.

Enhances collagen synthesis: Curcumin has been shown to promote collagen synthesis, which is essential for the repair and maintenance of connective tissues, including those affected in frozen shoulder, thus supporting recovery.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal tea for frozen shoulder due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling in the affected area.
Leaves: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric leaves, has been shown to have potential therapeutic effects on joint inflammation, making it a useful addition to medicinal tea for frozen shoulder.

Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal tea for frozen shoulder.

Seeds: Turmeric seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including joint pain and inflammation, making them a potential addition to medicinal tea for frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water in a pot, then add 1 tablespoon of grated fresh turmeric and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried ginger powder and 1 cinnamon stick to the pot, allow it to steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then mix in 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice for taste.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day, for at least a week for best results.
Discard any leftover tea after 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and pain associated with this condition. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which is native to Australia and has been used for centuries by indigenous people to treat a variety of ailments. The anti-inflammatory compounds in the tea, such as tannins and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation in the shoulder joint, which can help improve mobility and reduce pain. Additionally, the tea may also help to increase blood flow to the shoulder, which can further aid in the healing process. Drinking Tasmanian blue gum tea regularly may help to alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder and promote overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tea contains active constituents such as eucalyptus oil, which helps reduce inflammation in the shoulder joint, providing pain relief and promoting mobility.
Analgesic: The presence of compounds like 1,8-cineole in blue gum tea provides a numbing effect, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
Muscle relaxant: The relaxant properties of Tasmanian blue gum tea, attributed to its volatile oils, help soothe muscle tension, allowing for improved shoulder mobility and reduced stiffness.
Antispasmodic: Blue gum tea's antispasmodic properties, derived from its active constituents, aid in preventing muscle spasms and cramps, offering relief for those with frozen shoulder.
Circulatory stimulant: Tasmanian blue gum tea promotes blood flow and circulation, ensuring nutrients reach the affected shoulder joint, thereby supporting healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for reducing inflammation and swelling in frozen shoulder.
Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian blue gum tree contains tannins that have astringent properties, which can help alleviate pain and promote healing in frozen shoulder.
Seeds: The seeds of the Tasmanian blue gum tree contain essential oils that have been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, making them useful for treating frozen shoulder.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum tree contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them helpful in reducing pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5-10 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of crushed Tasmanian blue gum leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves and allow it to cool.
Drink 2-3 cups of this herbal tea daily for several weeks to help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Teaberry tea helps with frozen shoulder because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area. The tea's natural pain-relieving properties may also help alleviate the discomfort and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, teaberry tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and promote healing. Drinking teaberry tea regularly may help improve mobility and flexibility in the shoulder, making it easier to perform daily activities.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Teaberry tea contains compounds called tannins and volatile oils that have pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.

Anti-inflammatory: The methyl salicylate and other compounds found in teaberry leaves have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the shoulder joint, thereby improving mobility.
Antispasmodic: The relaxing and antispasmodic properties of teaberry tea can help relieve muscle spasms and tension in the shoulder muscles, promoting relaxation and reducing stiffness.

Circulatory stimulant: Teaberry tea has a stimulating effect on blood flow, which can help increase circulation to the shoulder joint, bringing oxygen and nutrients to the affected area to promote healing.
Diaphoretic: Teaberry tea has diaphoretic properties, which means it can help promote sweating and detoxification, supporting the body's natural healing processes and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the teaberry tea plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them an ideal part of the plant to use for making medicinal tea for frozen shoulder.

Bark: The bark of the teaberry tea plant contains tannins and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it a valuable part of the plant for making medicinal tea for frozen shoulder.

Roots: The roots of the teaberry tea plant contain a variety of beneficial compounds, including alkaloids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the shoulders of individuals suffering from frozen shoulder.

Buds: The buds of the teaberry tea plant are rich in essential oils and other beneficial compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the shoulders of individuals suffering from frozen shoulder.

Seeds: The seeds of the teaberry tea plant contain a variety of beneficial compounds, including fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the shoulders of individuals suffering from frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried teaberry leaves, bringing them to a boil.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in 1-2 teaspoons of honey for added flavor and potential anti-inflammatory benefits.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, for relief of frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tea helps with frozen shoulder because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb's active compounds, such as harpagoside, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making it an effective natural remedy for frozen shoulder. By promoting blood flow and reducing stiffness in the shoulder joint, devil's claw tea can help improve mobility and alleviate the discomfort associated with this condition. Its soothing effects on the musculoskeletal system make it a popular choice among those seeking a natural and holistic approach to managing frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Devil's claw tea contains harpagoside, which helps reduce inflammation in the shoulder joint, providing pain relief and improved mobility.
Analgesic: The iridoid glycosides in devil's claw, such as harpagoside, have pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
Fever-reducing: The presence of bitter compounds in devil's claw, like harpagoside and harpagide, can help reduce fever and inflammation, promoting overall shoulder health.
Muscle relaxant: Devil's claw tea can help relax muscles around the shoulder joint, reducing tension and improving range of motion, thanks to its content of glycosides and flavonoids.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of devil's claw, due to its phenolic acids and flavonoids, can help protect shoulder tissues from oxidative stress and support overall joint health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the devil's claw plant contain harpagosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the plant also contain harpagosides and are used in medicinal tea to help improve joint mobility and reduce stiffness in frozen shoulder.
Leaves: The leaves of the devil's claw plant are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected shoulder joint.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried devil's claw root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solid remains.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume 1-2 cups of the tea daily, as needed, for relief of frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Immortelle tea helps with frozen shoulder because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds, such as antioxidants and flavonoids, that work to reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected shoulder joint. These compounds also help to improve blood flow and stimulate the production of synovial fluid, which lubricates the joint and promotes mobility. Additionally, immortelle tea is known to have analgesic properties, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder. Drinking immortelle tea regularly can help to reduce stiffness, improve range of motion, and promote overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Imortelle tea helps reduce inflammation in the shoulder, providing pain relief and improved mobility, due to its high content of chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol.
Analgesic: The tea's ability to alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder is attributed to the presence of matricin, which is converted to anti-inflammatory compounds upon steam distillation.
Antispasmodic: Imortelle tea helps relax muscles, reducing tension and stiffness in the shoulder, thanks to the presence of chamazulene and bisabolol oxides A and B.
Circulation-boosting: The tea promotes blood flow to the affected area, aiding in the healing process and reducing stiffness, due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Imortelle tea's high antioxidant content, including rosmarinic acid and apigenin, helps protect cells from damage, supporting overall tissue health and accelerating recovery from frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Immmortelle tea made from the flowers is known to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating frozen shoulder.
Leaves: The leaves of the immortelle tea plant are rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in cases of frozen shoulder.
Buds: The buds of the immortelle tea plant are known for their pain-relieving properties, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for frozen shoulder.
Stems: The stems of the immortelle tea plant contain compounds that can improve circulation, which can help alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder.

Roots: The roots of the immortelle tea plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including frozen shoulder, due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried immortelle flowers, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
In a separate pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried willow bark, simmering for 15 minutes.
After 10 minutes, strain the immortelle tea and combine it with the willow bark decoction.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey and stir until fully dissolved.
Drink the tea twice a day, once in the morning and once in the evening, for a minimum of three weeks.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frozen shoulder with herbal teas is by consuming warm compresses of ginger and turmeric tea three times a day. These teas have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the shoulder joint. Additionally, chamomile tea can be consumed before bed to promote relaxation and improve sleep, which is essential for the healing process. Regular consumption of these herbal teas, combined with physical therapy, can effectively manage the symptoms of frozen shoulder.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal teas include shoulder stiffness and inflammation. For instance, chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, making it an ideal remedy for shoulder-related pains. Likewise, ginger tea can help alleviate joint stiffness and pain due to its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. Willow bark tea is another popular choice, as it contains salicin, the active ingredient in aspirin, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve pain. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any herbal tea regimen, especially for those with underlying health conditions or taking medications.
